stimulus: The following text is from a fictional 1925 short story. Mrs. Hayes had always been fond of china teapots, she said, and every year her sister’s birthday gift to her was a teapot of some kind—whatever the shopkeeper recommended, tagged at about eight or ten dollars.
stem: As used in the text, what does the word “tagged” most nearly mean?
rationale: Choice A is correct because in this context, "tagged at about eight or ten dollars" means the teapot was priced at that amount. Choice B is incorrect because while "tagged" can mean labeled, the dollar amount specifically refers to price. Choice C is incorrect because staining doesn’t fit a dollar amount. Choice D is incorrect because hiding doesn’t fit either.
